sentences.en: I think I did it better today than yesterday, and that makes me happy.
sentences.r: eojeboda oneul deo jalhan geot gataseo gippeoyo.

sentences.t: 여기 산 지 몇 년인데 아직도 도시도 뭐도 잘 모르는 것 같아요.
sentences.en: I've been here for years and I still feel like I don't know the city or anything.
sentences.r: yeogi san ji myeot nyeoninde ajikdo dosido mwodo jal moreuneun geot gatayo.

sentences.t: 직진하다가 오른쪽으로 돌면 호텔이 가까워요, 왼쪽에요.
sentences.en: Go straight, turn right, and the hotel is close, on the left.
sentences.r: jikjinhadaga oreunjjogeuro dolmyeon hoteri gakkawoyo, oenjjogeyo.

sentences.t: 드디어 찾았을 때 계속 가까이 있었다는 걸 깨달았어요.
sentences.en: When I finally found it, I realized it had been close the whole time.
sentences.r: deudieo chajasseul ttae gyesok gakkai isseotdaneun geol kkaedarasseoyo.

sentences.t: 마르코는 하루 십 분이면 된다는데, 매일 빠짐없이 해야 한대요.
sentences.en: Marco says you only need ten minutes a day, but you have to do them every single day.
sentences.r: raenggwineun haru sip bunimyeon dwendaneunde, maeil ppajimeopsi haeya handaeyo.

sentences.t: 저한테 물어보면 매일 연습하는 게 제일 도움 된다고 할 거예요.
sentences.en: If you asked me, I'd tell you that practicing every day is what helps the most.
sentences.r: jeohante mureobomyeon maeil yeonseuphaneun ge jeil doum dwendago hal geoyeyo.

sentences.t: 이 나이가 되니까 이제 다투기 싫고, 그냥 맛있는 커피 한 잔이 당겨요.
sentences.en: At my age I don't feel like arguing anymore, I just feel like a good coffee.
sentences.r: i naiga doenikka ije datugi sirko, geunyang masinneun keopi han jani danggyeoyo.
